channel.title: Nature Communications
channel.description:
Nature Communications is an open access, multidisciplinary journal dedicated to publishing high-quality research in all areas of the biological, health, physical, chemical and Earth sciences. Papers published by the journal aim to represent important advances of significance to specialists within each field.

We are committed to providing an efficient service for both authors and readers. Our team of independent editors make rapid and fair publication decisions. Prompt dissemination of accepted papers to a wide readership and beyond is achieved through a programme of continuous online publication.
channel.dc:rights: © 2025 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.
channel.prism:copyright: © 2025 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.

item.title: Machine learning-assisted kinetic matching model for rational electrode design in aqueous zinc-ion batteries
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 25 December 2025; doi:10.1038/s41467-025-67996-8

Aqueous zinc-ion batteries are safe and affordable but limited by incompatible electrode kinetics. Here, authors present a machine learning framework that resolves this mismatch, enabling the rational design of durable and stretchable zinc-ion batteries.
item.dc:title: Machine learning-assisted kinetic matching model for rational electrode design in aqueous zinc-ion batteries
item.dc:creator: Qian Xie
Yurong You
Dingshuo Qiao
Huan Xia
Hanning Zhang
Song-Zhu Kure-Chu
Tomasz Wejrzanowski
Tao Shui
Wei Zhang
ZhengMing Sun

item.title: Ultrasensitive multi-degree-of-freedom piezoionic sensor via synergistic hydrogel-ion interactions
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 25 December 2025; doi:10.1038/s41467-025-67613-8

Piezoionic flex-sensors are limited by sensitivity and unidirectional bending response. Here, the authors achieve a high piezoionic flex-sensitivity by tuning hydrogel-ion interactions, and decoupling of multi-degree-of-freedom bending via a square prism-shaped fiber structure.
item.dc:title: Ultrasensitive multi-degree-of-freedom piezoionic sensor via synergistic hydrogel-ion interactions
item.dc:creator: Yue Fang
Hao Ouyang
Yin Cheng
Yi Zhou
Liangjing Shi
Jing Sun
Ghim Wei Ho
Ranran Wang

item.title: Momentum space AC Josephson effect and intervalley coherence in multilayer graphene
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 24 December 2025; doi:10.1038/s41467-025-67838-7

The authors propose that when an intervalley coherent (IVC) state is subjected to a static magnetic field, it develops an oscillating orbital magnetization at microwave frequency, an effect analogous to a Josephson oscillation in momentum space. The effect could provide an experimental diagnostic for identifying the IVC state.
item.dc:title: Momentum space AC Josephson effect and intervalley coherence in multilayer graphene
item.dc:creator: Mainak Das
Chunli Huang

item.title: Directional flows using capillary assembly of photo-deformable colloidal particles at water-air interfaces
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 24 December 2025; doi:10.1038/s41467-025-67739-9

Colloidal particles experience capillary interactions at liquid interfaces, but modifying these interactions is challenging as shape change is required. Here, the authors report polymer particles that change shape with polarised light, and therefore create flow patterns with unusual paths.
item.dc:title: Directional flows using capillary assembly of photo-deformable colloidal particles at water-air interfaces
item.dc:creator: David Urban
Marcel Rey
Antonio Ciarlo
Marie Friederike Schulte
Emiliano Descrovi
Giovanni Volpe
